user.title: Pick a YKK SCK lock
user.description: Choose the lock you want to add. Only locks currently broadcasting BLE adverts visible to Home Assistant are listed.

adapters.title: Pick Bluetooth adapters for each role
adapters.description:
**Long-range** (read-only): scanner used to listen for the lock's state broadcasts. Any scanner that hears the lock will do — pick one with a stable signal.

**Short-range** (read/write): scanner used to send lock / unlock commands. Must be physically close to the lock (≤ ~3 m) to bond reliably. Typically the ESPHome `bluetooth_proxy` near the door.

Leave either as **Auto** if you only have one adapter today, or want HA to pick the best available scanner.

register.title: Register with the lock
register.description:
About to register with **{name}** (`{address}`).

1. Walk up to the lock so the Home Assistant Bluetooth adapter (or a nearby `bluetooth_proxy`) has line-of-sight (under ~3 m).
2. **Press and hold the physical registration button on the lock** until it enters registration mode.
3. Choose a 6-digit PIN below, then submit immediately — the lock leaves registration mode after a short window.

The handshake captures this lock's unique advertising key; without it, ongoing state cannot be decoded.

data.name: Smartphone name shown on the lock

abort.no_devices_found: No YKK SCK locks are currently advertising. Make sure HA's Bluetooth adapter (or a bluetooth_proxy) is close enough to the lock.
abort.unsupported_platform: Home Assistant is running on a platform the underlying Bleak library does not support (Android). Use a Linux/macOS/Windows host or an ESPHome bluetooth_proxy.

error.registration_failed: Registration failed. Make sure the lock is in registration mode (physical button) and try again.
error.timeout: The lock did not respond in time. Move closer and retry while the lock is in registration mode.
error.unknown: Unexpected error — check the Home Assistant logs.

init.title: Bluetooth adapters
init.description: Pick which scanner handles each role. Scanners currently seeing the lock are listed by RSSI; sources you previously selected remain selectable even when not currently visible.
